task_description: Screen each abstract for relevance to: machine learning applications in biomedical research. Label RELEVANT if the paper directly applies machine learning methods to a biomedical problem involving human health, disease, or biological data. Label NOT_RELEVANT otherwise. Note: papers applying ML to non-human domains, administrative tasks, or general methodology without biomedical application should be labelled NOT_RELEVANT.

papers.title: Variational Autoencoder for Latent Representation Learning in Single-Cell RNA Sequencing
papers.abstract: We train a variational autoencoder on single-cell RNA sequencing data from 1.2 million human peripheral blood mononuclear cells to learn low-dimensional cell-type embeddings. Downstream clustering using the learned latent space recovers 28 cell types with adjusted Rand index 0.91 versus expert annotations. The representations generalise to five held-out cohorts with different library preparation protocols.
papers.topic_hint: VAE, single-cell RNA-seq, cell-type discovery, dimensionality reduction, genomics
papers.methodology_hint: variational autoencoder, scRNA-seq, adjusted Rand index 0.91, cross-cohort generalisation
papers.claimed_contribution: VAE for scRNA-seq cell-type representation generalising across five cohorts with ARI 0.91.

papers.title: Machine Learning for Predictive Maintenance in Industrial Wind Turbines
papers.abstract: We apply gradient boosting and LSTM models to SCADA sensor streams from 240 offshore wind turbines to predict gearbox failures 72 hours in advance. Precision 0.84 and recall 0.79 are achieved on a held-out 12-month test period. Early warning lead time reduces unplanned downtime by 31% in retrospective evaluation.
papers.topic_hint: predictive maintenance, wind turbines, SCADA, industrial IoT, gradient boosting
papers.methodology_hint: gradient boosting, LSTM, SCADA sensors, precision 0.84, recall 0.79, downtime reduction
papers.claimed_contribution: ML framework predicting wind turbine gearbox failure 72 hours ahead, reducing downtime by 31%.

papers.title: Graph Transformer for Drug Synergy Prediction in Cancer Cell Lines
papers.abstract: We develop a graph transformer that encodes drug molecular structures as graphs and predicts synergy scores for drug pairs tested against 39 cancer cell lines from the ALMANAC dataset. The model achieves Pearson r=0.81 and outperforms random forest, GNN, and DeepSynergy baselines. Cross-cell-line generalisation is validated on six held-out cancer types.
papers.topic_hint: graph transformer, drug synergy, cancer cell lines, ALMANAC, computational oncology
papers.methodology_hint: graph transformer, ALMANAC, Pearson 0.81, RF/GNN/DeepSynergy baselines, cross-cell-line validation
papers.claimed_contribution: Graph transformer for drug synergy prediction with Pearson 0.81, generalising to six held-out cancer types.

papers.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Autonomous Navigation in Unstructured Outdoor Terrain
papers.abstract: We train a deep RL agent using proximal policy optimisation to navigate wheeled robots across unstructured outdoor terrain using RGB-D sensor input. The agent achieves 88% goal-reach rate on held-out terrains versus 61% for a hand-engineered baseline. Sim-to-real transfer is demonstrated on a physical platform in three outdoor environments.
papers.topic_hint: reinforcement learning, robot navigation, outdoor terrain, sim-to-real, robotics
papers.methodology_hint: PPO, RGB-D navigation, goal-reach rate 88%, sim-to-real transfer, physical robot
papers.claimed_contribution: RL agent for outdoor robot navigation with 88% goal-reach rate and successful sim-to-real transfer.

papers.title: Convolutional Neural Network for Somatic Copy Number Alteration Detection from Shallow WGS
papers.abstract: We develop a CNN that processes read depth profiles from shallow whole-genome sequencing to call somatic copy number alterations in tumour samples at 0.1x coverage. The model achieves precision 0.88 and recall 0.85 on a validation set of 1,400 tumour-normal pairs, substantially outperforming segmentation-based baselines. Low-coverage inference enables cost-effective liquid biopsy applications.
papers.topic_hint: CNN, copy number alteration, whole-genome sequencing, liquid biopsy, somatic mutation
papers.methodology_hint: CNN, shallow WGS, 0.1x coverage, precision 0.88, recall 0.85, segmentation baseline comparison
papers.claimed_contribution: CNN for somatic copy number detection at 0.1x WGS coverage, enabling cost-effective liquid biopsy.

papers.title: Transformer-Based Code Generation for Automated Software Refactoring
papers.abstract: We fine-tune a code-generation transformer on a dataset of 1.8 million paired code before/after refactoring commits to automate code smell removal. The model achieves CodeBLEU of 0.73 and reduces code complexity metrics by 22% on a held-out benchmark. Human evaluation confirms 79% of suggestions are accepted by developers.
papers.topic_hint: code generation, software refactoring, transformer, CodeBLEU, software engineering
papers.methodology_hint: code transformer fine-tuning, 1.8M refactoring commits, CodeBLEU 0.73, developer acceptance
papers.claimed_contribution: Code generation transformer automating refactoring with 79% developer acceptance rate.

papers.title: Sparse Autoencoder for Metagenomics Feature Extraction and Microbiome Disease Association
papers.abstract: We apply a sparse autoencoder to shotgun metagenomic sequencing data from 3,200 gut microbiome samples to extract interpretable microbial community features. Features learned from healthy controls and colorectal cancer patients separate disease states with AUC 0.87 and identify known oncogenic taxa. The approach generalises to two independent validation cohorts.
papers.topic_hint: sparse autoencoder, metagenomics, microbiome, colorectal cancer, disease association
papers.methodology_hint: sparse autoencoder, shotgun metagenomics, AUC 0.87, colorectal cancer, two validation cohorts
papers.claimed_contribution: Sparse autoencoder for metagenomics features achieving AUC 0.87 for colorectal cancer detection.

papers.title: Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Traffic Signal Optimisation in Smart Cities
papers.abstract: We train a cooperative multi-agent RL system to coordinate traffic signal timing across 120 intersections in a simulated urban network. Average vehicle delay is reduced by 34% versus fixed-time signals and 18% versus adaptive single-agent baselines. The system adapts to demand fluctuations without manual re-tuning.
papers.topic_hint: multi-agent RL, traffic signal control, smart city, urban transportation
papers.methodology_hint: cooperative MARL, 120-intersection simulation, delay reduction 34%, adaptive baseline comparison
papers.claimed_contribution: Cooperative MARL for traffic signal coordination reducing vehicle delay by 34% over fixed-time control.

papers.title: Attention-Based Neural Network for CRISPR Guide RNA Off-Target Effect Prediction
papers.abstract: We develop an attention mechanism-based neural network trained on 420,000 CRISPR-Cas9 off-target cleavage measurements to predict guide RNA specificity scores. The model achieves AUROC 0.93 on a held-out benchmark of 15,000 guide-target pairs, outperforming CFD score, DeepCRISPR, and CRISPOR baselines. In-silico guide selection using the model reduces predicted off-target sites by 3.2x.
papers.topic_hint: attention neural network, CRISPR, guide RNA, off-target prediction, gene editing
papers.methodology_hint: attention NN, 420k cleavage measurements, AUROC 0.93, CFD/DeepCRISPR/CRISPOR baselines
papers.claimed_contribution: Attention NN for CRISPR off-target prediction with AUROC 0.93, reducing predicted off-targets 3.2x.

papers.title: Contrastive Learning for Visual Product Similarity in E-Commerce Recommendation
papers.abstract: We apply SimCLR-style contrastive pre-training on 50 million product images to learn visual embeddings for e-commerce similarity search and recommendation. Top-10 recall improves by 14% over supervised classification pre-training on a held-out test set of 500,000 query-product pairs. A/B testing confirms a 6% click-through rate lift in online deployment.
papers.topic_hint: contrastive learning, product similarity, e-commerce, visual search, recommendation
papers.methodology_hint: SimCLR contrastive pre-training, 50M product images, top-10 recall, A/B testing, click-through lift
papers.claimed_contribution: Contrastive visual embeddings for e-commerce improving top-10 recall 14% and CTR 6% in deployment.
